violations.text: Various food was observed not properly date marked after being frozen. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Various food products were observed past the date of disposition. Per PIC, establishment freezes food products and defrosts in walk-in cooler. Proper date marking after frozen was reviewed with staff. Proper dates added to food at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/13/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils ice machine and can opener were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was moved to 3-bay sink at the time of inspection. Ice machine was unplugged to let ice burn, and an out of order sign was placed on machine at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to use ice machine until it is properly sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Ground beef and beef, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Ground beef was observed at 46 DF and beef was observed between 52-59 DF. Food was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Establishment is to mark all spray bottles with the common name of chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to continue to work with pest control and is to have a report for inspector at the time of next inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025
Various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025
Inspector observed openings to lids on dumpster. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2024

Dead rodent observed outside of building near grease disposal. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies.PIC removed and disposed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2024
Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (salsa) in the prep unit cooler. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings on the floor next to the grease trap. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The door to the outside, located in the dining room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/07/2023
At the time of inspection soup was observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen area. At the time of inspection single service items were stored inverted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The floors in the ice machine room are constructed of wood which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2024
